one_liner: Vercel's REST API and TypeScript SDK let developers manage deployments, projects, and domains using self-serve Bearer access tokens, with an official pattern for building/hosting MCP servers on Vercel Functions.

auth_methods.notes: Access Token used as Bearer token in Authorization header for REST API and SDK; user-generated via account settings (Sign in with Vercel Tokens).
OIDC-based tokens used for Vercel Connect, integrations, and service-to-service auth contexts (not the primary REST API auth path).

self_serve_notes: Developers can generate personal access tokens directly from their Vercel account dashboard for free (Hobby plan); no approval process needed for basic API access, though some enterprise-grade features require Pro/Enterprise plans.

api_surface.notes: Covers deployments, projects, domains, DNS, integrations/marketplace, environment variables, teams, and more; also offers an official TypeScript SDK wrapping the REST API.

evidence.supports: Describes Access Token, ID Token, and Refresh Token mechanics used for authentication.
Shows SDK initialization with bearerToken for authenticating API calls.
Confirms free Hobby plan available for self-serve access including CLI and Git integrations.
Details REST API surface for integrations/marketplace functionality.
Describes OIDC token usage for service-to-service and integration contexts.

flags: No dedicated official Vercel-hosted MCP server product found; docs describe how to build/host your own MCP server on Vercel infrastructure rather than an existing first-party MCP offering.
Some advanced enterprise features (SSO, HIPAA) require paid Pro/Enterprise plans, but core API access is free.
